The Waiting Restrictions (Boyne Bridge Place, Belfast – Pick-up and Drop-off Point) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places on Roads (Coaches) (Glengall Street, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Allocation of Housing and Homelessness (Eligibility) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the Allocation of Housing and Homelessness (Eligibility) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 397) to ensure that persons who were residing in a country or territory and who left that country or territory when His Majesty’s Government issued public information to advise British nationals to leave that country or territory, or arranged the evacuation of British nationals from that country or territory, are exempt from the habitual residence test otherwise applicable in relation to eligibility for an allocation of housing accommodation and for housing assistance.

items.title: The Social Security (Habitual Residence, Past Presence and Temporary Absence) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Part 1 of the Regulations provides for citation, commencement and interpretation.

items.title: The Planning Fees (Deemed Planning Applications and Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Planning Fees (Deemed Planning Applications and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 by uplifting applicable fees.

items.title: The Penalty Charges (Prescribed Devices) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Road Races (Bushwhacker Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Companies (Disqualification Orders) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made in consequence of the insertion by section 32 of and Schedule 13 to the Finance Act 2024, of new sections 8ZF and 8ZG both of which extend to Northern Ireland, into the Company Directors Disqualification Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”).

items.title: The Public Interest Disclosure (Prescribed Persons) (Amendment No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Schedule to the Public Interest Disclosure (Prescribed Persons) Order (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 401). It updates the reference to the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy and adds a reference to the Treasury as a prescribed person. The description of matters for the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, the Secretary of State for Transport, and the Treasury now reflects newly prescribed matters concerning sanctions-related functions of these prescribed persons.

items.title: The Eel Fishing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Eel Fishing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010.

items.title: The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for winter fuel payments.

items.title: The Legal Complaints and Regulation (2016 Act) (Commencement No.1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order brings into operation the Legal Complaints and Regulation Act (Northern Ireland) 2016, other than sections 9, 19(2)(c), 38(2)(c) and paragraph 2(4) of Schedule 4 to that Act, on 1st September 2025.

items.title: The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places on Roads, Loading Bay and Waiting Restrictions (Newcastle) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Carneyhaugh Business Park, Newry (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Housing Benefit (Habitual Residence) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 10 of the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”). Regulation 10 provides that an individual claiming housing benefit who is not habitually resident in the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands, the Isle of Man or the Republic of Ireland is a “person from abroad” and is treated as not having a liability to make payments in respect of the dwelling which he occupies as his home. As a result, such an individual is not entitled to housing benefit. Regulation 10(5) specifies the circumstances in which an individual is not considered a “person from abroad” when claiming housing benefit.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Coronavirus Act 2020 (Registration of Deaths and Still-Births) (Extension) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Section 18(3) of, and Part 3 of Schedule 13 to, the Coronavirus Act 2020 contain provisions relating to the registration of deaths and still-births in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Coronavirus Act 2020 (Extension of Provisions Relating to Live Links for Courts and Tribunals) (No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order extends the operation of the provisions in the Coronavirus Act 2020 (c. 7) which allow the wider and extended use of video or audio live links by all or any courts or a statutory tribunal (Part 1 of Schedule 27). It extends the provisions, which facilitate public participation in legal proceedings if, and when live links are used while protecting those proceedings from unauthorised recordings or transmission (Part 2 of Schedule 27).

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The U324 Cannyreagh Road, Donaghadee (Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Mill Avenue, Belfast (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The U1105 Killybracken Road, Dungannon (Abandonment and Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Recovery of Health Services Charges (Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Recovery of Health Services Charges (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 provides for a scheme for the recovery of charges in cases where an injured person who receives a compensation payment in respect of his injury has received hospital treatment or ambulance services. The charges are specified in certificates issued by the Department for Communities and are payable by persons who pay compensation to the injured person. The charges in each case are calculated by reference to a tariff of charges provided for by the Recovery of Health Services Charges (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Control of Traffic (Island Street Area, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Kinard Park, Garvagh (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Prohibition of Waiting (Schools) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Agricultural Schemes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend

items.title: The Farming for Sustainability Innovation Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Regulations establish the requirements for hosting payments for innovation farm visits and eligibility criteria of innovation visits outside Northern Ireland provided by the Department.

items.title: The Student Fees (Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Regulations 2 to 4 of these regulations amend the Student Fees (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) by increasing the basic and higher amounts prescribed in the 2005 Regulations. In each case the increases are by 2.7% and will come into operation on 1st September 2026.

items.title: The Farm Sustainability Standards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Cross Compliance formerly linked payments to farmers under the Common Agricultural Policy to compliance with a range of laws and standards known as Good Agricultural and Environmental Conditions and Statutory Management Requirements (SMR). The new Farm Sustainability Standards replace these former cross compliance controls in relation to all breaches of the new underpinning requirements which have occurred from 1st January 2026.

items.title: The Enforcement of Fines and Other Penalties (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These regulations uplift the charges that shall be levied in respect of the removal, storage and disposal of vehicles seized under a vehicle seizure order, by amending the charges set out in Schedule 3 to the Enforcement of Fines and Other Penalties Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2018.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations uplift the charges that shall be levied in respect of the removal and storage of vehicles seized because they have been driven carelessly, inconsiderately or illegally off-road and is causing, or is likely to cause, alarm, distress or annoyance to members of the public.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations uplift the charges that shall be levied in respect of the removal and storage of vehicles seized because they have been driven without insurance, by amending the charges set out in Regulation 6 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Social Fund (Child Funeral Fund) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the amount paid under the Child Funeral Fund as part of the social fund.

items.title: The Health and Social Care Pension Scheme (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Health and Social Care Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (S.R. 2015 No. 120) (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Recognised Professional Bodies) (Revocation of Recognition) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Insolvency Practitioners (Recognised Professional Bodies) Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 declares the bodies specified in the Schedule to that Order to be recognised professional bodies for the purposes of Article 350 of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989. The Law Society of Northern Ireland has asked to have their recognition as recognised professional body revoked.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1981 (S.R. 1981 No. 225) to—

items.title: The Health and Social Care (Exemptions from Charges, Payments and Remission of Charges) (Amendment and Transitional Provision) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Optical Charges and Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the Optical Charges Regulations”), the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the Travelling Expenses Regulations”) and the General Ophthalmic Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the Ophthalmic Regulations”).

items.title: The Damages for Bereavement (Variation of Sum) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which applies to causes of action accruing on or after 1st December 2025, amends Article 3A(3) of the Fatal Accidents (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 (S.I. 1977/1251 (N.I. 18)) increasing the sum which may be awarded for damages for bereavement in Northern Ireland from £17,200 to £19,700.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Derry/Londonderry) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Occupational Pension Schemes (Collective Money Purchase Schemes) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Regulation 2 amends the Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Consultation by Employers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 to correct a minor drafting error in regulation 10 made by the Occupational Pension Schemes (Collective Money Purchase Schemes) (Modifications and Consequential and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2022 (S.R. 2022 No. 192).

items.title: The Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (Commencement No. 18) (Abolition of Benefits) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order is a further stage in the replacement of six benefits with universal credit (“UC”).

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Amendment and Transitional Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insolvency Practitioners Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the 2006 Regulations”).

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Wet Wipes Containing Plastic) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations prohibit persons from supplying or offering to supply wet wipes containing plastic (see definitions in regulation 2). The Regulations apply only in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Farm Sustainability Payment Scheme (Eligibility etc) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations establish the requirements for farm sustainability payments provided by the Department. The payments made by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs are to applicants who are eligible for farm sustainability payments.

items.title: The Waste (Materials Facilities) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Waste Management Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”) and the Pollution Prevention and Control (Industrial Emissions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”). The amendments require the Department to ensure that appropriate conditions are included in waste management licences and PPC (IE) permits which authorise materials facilities so that the new Code of Practice on Sampling and Reporting at Materials Facilities issued by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs on 31st January 2025 (the “Materials Facilities Code”) is complied with. The Regulations insert new definitions of various terms relating to the materials facilities into the 2003 Regulations and the 2013 Regulations.

items.title: The Belvoir Road, Belfast (Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Social Security (Residence in an EEA State or Switzerland) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Regulation 1 provides for citation, commencement and interpretation.

items.title: The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/1211) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies appropriate revaluation percentages. The percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 80 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993. The lower revaluation percentage introduced by the Pensions (No. 2) Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (c. 13 (N.I.)) does not apply to pensionable service before 6th April 2009.

items.title: The State Pension Revaluation for Transitional Pensions Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/1219) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under sections 148AC(3) and 189(1) and (4) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) (“the 1992 Act”), specifies the “revaluing percentage” as 39.0 per cent.

items.title: The State Pension Debits and Credits (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/1220) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under sections 148AD(2) and (3) and 189(1) and (4) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5).

items.title: The Government Resources and Accounts Act (Northern Ireland) 2001 (Estimates and Accounts) (Designation of Bodies) (Amendment) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Government Resources and Accounts (Northern Ireland) Act 2001 (Estimates and Accounts) (Designation of Bodies) Order 2025 (S.I. 2025 No.50) (“the principal Order”), which designates specified central government bodies in relation to named government departments for the purpose of those departments’ supply estimates and resource accounts.

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Recognised Professional Bodies) (Revocation of Recognition) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Insolvency Practitioners (Recognised Professional Bodies) Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 declares the bodies specified in the Schedule to that Order to be recognised professional bodies for the purposes of Article 350 of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989. The Institute of Chartered Accountants in Ireland (otherwise known as the Chartered Accountants Ireland) has asked to have their recognition as recognised professional body revoked.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pensions Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Farm Sustainability Standards (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made in exercise of the powers conferred by sections 48 and 50(3)(a) of, and paragraph 5 of Schedule 6 to, the Agriculture Act2020 (c.21)in order to make miscellaneous amendments and corrections to The Farm Sustainability Standards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025 (SR2025 No.165).

items.title: The Parking Places on Roads (Coaches) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Waiting Restrictions (Springfield Road, Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The U5390 Church Hill, Jonesborough (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 for the purpose of increasing the tariff for periodic support payments made under the Scheme to accredited small and medium biomass installations. The increases are for heat generated from the date these Regulations come into operation. These Regulations fix the tariffs for these installations for heat generated up to the end of March 2026, and those tariffs will then be adjusted in line with increases or decreases in the consumer prices index to give the tariffs for later financial years. These Regulations also alter the zero tariff for heat produced in excess of the (unchanged) initial heat allowance for a medium biomass installation, and set a cap of 400,000kWhth for a medium biomass installation. The Regulations also include a saving provision in respect of heat generated before these regulations come into operation.

items.title: The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Legal Aid for Crown Court Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the Legal Aid for Crown Court Proceedings (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2005 which prescribe the remuneration for solicitors and counsel assigned under Articles 29 or 36(2) of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Civil Legal Services (Remuneration) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order prescribes the remuneration payable to solicitors and counsel for providing civil legal services under Part 2 of the Access to Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2003.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts and County Court Appeals (Criminal Legal Aid) (Costs) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts and County Court Appeals (Criminal Legal Aid) (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2009 which prescribe the remuneration for solicitors and counsel assigned under Articles 28, 28A or 30 of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Gaming (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order amends Articles 77(1), 106(3), 108(6) and 108(8) of The Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Northern Ireland) Order 1985 by increasing various monetary limits relating to prizes and charges. It also revokes provisions of previously made ‘Variation of Monetary Limits’ Orders.

items.title: The Further Education (Student Support) (Eligibility) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend legislation which makes provision about student support in the form of grants in respect of fees payable in connection with attendance on courses at an institution of further education.

items.title: The Schools (Part-Time 20mph Speed Limit) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Rates (Small Business Hereditament Relief) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of “qualifying year” in the Rates (Small Business Hereditament Relief) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 to provide for an extension of the small business rate relief scheme until 31st March 2026.

items.title: The Gaming (Gaming Machines in Registered Clubs) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations prescribe £400 for the purposes of Article 106(5) of The Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Northern Ireland) Order 1985, as the maximum prize in respect of any one game played by means of a gaming machine in a registered club.

items.title: The Emergency Fuel Payment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for payments (“Emergency Fuel Payments”) to be made to eligible persons under the Emergency Fuel Payment Scheme (Northern Ireland) 2025.

items.title: The Social Security (Income and Capital Disregards) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Rule amends various social security Regulations to ensure payments pursuant to the Ministry of Defence’s Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual and Transgender Financial Recognition Scheme (“the Scheme”) are to be disregarded indefinitely as capital and income for the purposes of calculating entitlement to income-related social security benefit.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Authorised Weight) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Authorised Weight) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”) to provide for an increase in the maximum authorised weights in domestic traffic for certain alternatively fuelled vehicles and certain zero-emission vehicles, to support their uptake as part of the move to a net zero target.

items.title: The Farm Sustainability Payments to Farmers (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made in exercise of the powers conferred by section 48 and 50(3)(a) of, and paragraph 5 of Schedule 6 to, the Agriculture Act 2020 (c.21).

items.title: The Rates (Regional Rates) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order fixes the amount of the regional rates for the year ending 31st March 2026. It fixes 29.89 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable net annual values of hereditaments (“non-domestic regional rate”) and 0.5294 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable capital values of hereditaments (“domestic regional rate”).

items.title: The Rates (Exemption for Automatic Telling Machines in Rural Areas) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Article 42(1F) and (1G) of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 (“the 1977 Order”) provide that there shall be distinguished in the NAV list as wholly exempt from rates any automatic telling machine which is situated in a rural area during a relevant year ending before such date as is specified by an Order made by the Department of Finance. 1st April 2026 is specified by this Order.

items.title: The Rates (Temporary Rebate) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Article 31D of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 provides for a rebate on occupied rates for certain retail properties.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the State Pension Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (S.R. 2003 No. 28) (“the 2003 Regulations”), the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 405) (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”), the Housing Benefit (Persons who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 406) (“the Pension Age Housing Benefit Regulations”), the Universal Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R. 2016 No. 216) (“the 2016 Regulations”) and the Universal Credit (Transitional Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2016 (S.R. 2016 No. 226) (“the 2016 Transitional Regulations”).

items.title: The Waste (Fees and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs publishes a charging scheme to ensure that as far as practical, fees and charges payable for waste management licenses under the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (the 1997 Order) are sufficient to recover costs for the Department in exercising its functions under the 1997 Order. However, charges for processing applications for registration as carriers, brokers or dealers in controlled waste are required to be set out in legislation. The purpose of these Regulations is to increase those charges to ensure the Department operates in accordance with the principles of Managing Puhlic Money Northern Ireland (MPMNI) by seeking full cost recovery. Details of the increased fees will also be included in the charging scheme which sets out the fees and charges payable in respect of waste management licenses.

items.title: The Moore’s Lane, Lurgan (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Education (Student Support, etc.) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend legislation which makes provision about, or in connection with, student finance. Three sets of Student Support Regulations are amended. Those Regulations are the Education (Student Support) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2009 “the 2009 Regulations”, the Student Fees (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 “the 2005 Regulations” and the Education (Student Support, etc.) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024.

items.title: The Farm Sustainability (Transitional Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations establish the requirements for farm sustainability transition payments and farm sustainability payments provided by the Department. The payments made by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs are to applicants who are eligible for farm sustainability transition payments and farm sustainability payments.

items.title: The Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the 2004 Order”) which specifies a number of persons or bodies as public authorities for certain purposes under Part VII of the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 (“the 1998 Order”).

items.title: The Police Trainee (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Trainee Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 to repeal the emergency provisions introduced in 2020 to allow the Chief Constable to impose different requirements as to the period of service and training for police trainees in response to COVID-19. This is in line with the commitment to retain the provisions for only as long as necessary to address the ongoing pandemic.

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 (S.I. 1979/925 (N.I.9)) (“the Order”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Order applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled before they died.

items.title: The Prohibition of Right-Hand Turn (Carrickfergus) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Pension Protection Fund and Occupational Pension Schemes (Levy Ceiling) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to provisions of an Order (S.I. 2025/103) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 178 of the Pensions Act 2004, specifies, for the purposes of Article 160 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 (“the 2005 Order”), the levy ceiling for the financial year beginning on 1st April 2025.

items.title: The Private Tenancies (2022 Act) (Commencement No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of section 7 of the Private Tenancies Act (Northern Ireland) 2022. These provisions relate to the regulation of rent, in particular, the restriction on the frequency of rent increases and, the requirement to give written notice of any increases.

items.title: The Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) 1980 (S.R. 1980 No. 346) to—

items.title: The Farming for Sustainability Knowledge Transfer Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Regulations establish the requirements for hosting and learner support payments provided by the Department. The payments made by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs are to applicants who are eligible for hosting and learner support payments.

items.title: The Suckler Cow Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations establish the Suckler Cow Scheme. The Scheme provides for payments to be made by the Department to eligible farm businesses in relation to age at first calving and calving intervals, which meet the eligible animal requirements.

items.title: The Teachers’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Teachers’ Pensions Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 (S.R. 2014 No. 310) (“the 2014 Regulations”).

items.title: The Rates (Social Sector Value) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Rates (Social Sector Value) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”) provide, subject to exceptions, for a person to be chargeable to rates in respect of a domestic property owned by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive or a housing association listed in Schedule 1 to the Regulations, on the basis of social sector value rather than on the basis of the property’s rateable capital value. In determining a social sector value, the Department of Finance is under a duty, by virtue of Article 23A(2) of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977, to ensure that the amount of rates chargeable is such proportion of any rent payable in respect of the property as the Department considers appropriate.

items.title: The Valuation (Telecommunications, Natural Gas and Water) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: The Valuation (Telecommunications, Natural Gas and Water) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010, (“the 2010 Regulations”), provide that certain hereditaments of any company listed in the Schedule shall, for the purposes of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977, be treated as a single hereditament occupied by that company.

items.title: The Emergency Fuel Payment (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Emergency Fuel Payment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025 (S.R. 2025 No. 23) (“the 2025 Regulations”) to correct an error in those Regulations.

items.title: The Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payment to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Direct Payments to Farmers (Cross-Compliance) (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made in exercise of the powers conferred by section 48 and 50(3)(a) of, and paragraph 5 of Schedule 6 to, the Agriculture Act 2020 (c.21) in order to make provision in assimilated direct legislation governing payments for nationally funded schemes.

items.title: The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Planning (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Planning (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 which prescribe the fee payable in respect of applications made under the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011. The Regulations increase planning fees by approximately 2.1% overall. Regulation 3 revokes the Planning (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024.

items.title: The Sydenham By-Pass, Belfast (Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Government Resources and Accounts Act (Northern Ireland) 2001 (Estimates and Accounts) (Designation of Bodies) Order 2025
items.description: This Order designates specified central government bodies in relation to named government departments for the purpose of those departments’ supply estimates and resource accounts.

items.title: The Traffic Weight Restriction (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/264) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 109(4) of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies 1.7 per cent. as the percentage by which that part of guaranteed minimum pensions attributable to earnings factors for the tax years 1988-1989 to 1996-1997, and payable by contracted-out, defined benefit occupational pension schemes, is to be increased.

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/255) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 148 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992. It directs that the earnings factors relevant to the calculation of additional pension in any long-term benefit (in practice, now relevant only to the calculation of inherited additional pension), or of any guaranteed minimum pension, or to any other calculation required under Part III of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 are to be increased for the tax years specified in the Schedule to this Order by the percentage of their amount specified in that Schedule. This is to ensure that earnings factors for those years are revalued at 2024-2025 earnings levels.

items.title: The Road Races (Tour of the Sperrins) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Public Service Pensions Revaluation Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Section 9 of the Public Service Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 (c.2) (revaluation) applies to pension schemes which, under that Act, require a revaluation of pensionable earnings, or a proportion of such earnings, accrued as a pension, by reference to a change in prices or earnings (or both) in a given period.

items.title: The Road Races (Croft Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Universal Credit) (Social Sector Size Criteria) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for welfare supplementary payments to mitigate the impact where a person is affected by the application of the social sector size criteria in the calculation of the housing costs element of universal credit. That is to say, where the number of bedrooms in a renter’s accommodation exceeds the number of bedrooms to which a renter is entitled in accordance with the regulations.

items.title: The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Extension) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend laws made under Article 137 and Article 137A of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015. Those laws provided for welfare supplementary payment to be paid to individuals where certain entitlements to social security benefits had been terminated or reduced. The relevant common feature of those laws was that they provided for welfare supplementary payment to end on 31st March 2025.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Derry/Londonderry) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Universal Credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations make further provision for welfare supplementary payments. These payments are designed to mitigate the impact of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (S.I. 2015/2006 (N.I. 1)) and the Welfare Reform and Work (Northern Ireland) Order 2016 (S.I. 2016/999 (N.I. 1)) referred to in “A Fresh Start: the Stormont Agreement and Implementation Plan”. These Regulations make provision to take account of the introduction of universal credit.

items.title: The Coronavirus Act 2020 (Extension of Provisions Relating to Live Links for Courts and Tribunals) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order extends the operation of the provisions in the Coronavirus Act 2020 (c. 7) which allow the wider and extended use of video or audio live links by all or any courts or a statutory tribunal (Part 1 of Schedule 27). It extends the provisions, which facilitate public participation in legal proceedings if, and when live links are used while protecting those proceedings from unauthorised recordings or transmission (Part 2 of Schedule 27).

items.title: The Coronavirus Act 2020 (Registration of Deaths and Still-Births) (Extension) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Section 18(3) of, and Part 3 of Schedule 13 to, the Coronavirus Act 2020 contain provisions relating to the registration of deaths and still-births in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order increases, from 6th April 2025, the limits applying to certain awards of industrial tribunals, the Fair Employment Tribunal or Labour Relations Agency statutory arbitration, and other amounts payable under employment legislation, as specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2025/295) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under sections 150, 150A, 151 and 151A of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, alters the rates and amounts of certain social security benefits and other sums.

items.title: The Road Races (Circuit of Ireland Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 to increase amounts of lump sums payable to people with diffuse mesothelioma or to their dependants under the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008. The increase in the amounts paid from 1st April 2025 in each case is 1.7 per cent, rounded up or down to the nearest £1 as appropriate.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provisions necessary to give full effect to the 2025 Up-rating and are consequential on the Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order (Northern Ireland) 2025 (the “Up-rating Order”). These Regulations make in relation to Northern Ireland only provision corresponding to provision contained in Regulations made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions in relation to Great Britain and accordingly, by virtue of section 149(3) of, and paragraph 10 of Schedule 5, to the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, are not subject to the requirement of section 149(2) of that Act for prior reference to the Social Security Advisory Committee.

items.title: The Registered Rents (Increase) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order increases the rents registered with the rent officer for Northern Ireland under Part IV of the Private Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 during the period beginning on 2nd April 2007 and ending on 8th June 2025 by 2.7 percent.

items.title: The Pensions Increase (Review) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Under section 69 of the Social Security Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1975 (c.15) (increase of official pensions), where the Department for Communities under section 132(1) of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c.8), directs that the sums which are the additional pensions in long-term benefits are to be increased by a specified percentage, the Department of Finance shall provide by order for the increase in the rates of public service pensions. The Pensions (Increase) Act (Northern Ireland) 1971 (c.35) defines certain terms and sets out when a pension “begins” (the day after the last day of service in respect of which the pension is payable) and how the increase applies to lump sums.

items.title: The U8017 (unnamed road) off Redrock Road, Markethill (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Recognition of Professional Qualifications and Implementation of International Recognition Agreements (Amendment) (Extension to Switzerland etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision under sections 3(1) and (2), and 13(1) of the Professional Qualifications Act 2022 (c. 20) (“the 2022 Act”) to implement provisions in the agreement between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Swiss Confederation on Recognition of Professional Qualifications done at London on 14th June 2023 (“the Switzerland Recognition of Professional Qualifications Agreement”).

items.title: The Road Races (Cookstown 100 Motor Cycle Road Race) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Road Races (Craigantlet Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Universal Credit, Personal Independence Payment, Jobseeker’s Allowance and Employment and Support Allowance (Claims and Payments) (Amendment and Modification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend and modify Schedule 5 (deductions from benefits and direct payment to third parties), to the Universal Credit, Personal Independence Payment, Jobseeker’s Allowance and Employment and Support Allowance (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R 2016 No. 220) (“the 2016 Regulations”).

items.title: The Road Races (Spamount Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Road Races (North West 200) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Road Races (Maiden City Stages Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places on Roads (Diplomatic Vehicles) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Northern Ireland Climate Commissioner Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 50 of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022. They establish the office of the Northern Ireland Climate Commissioner and make provision about the functioning of that office.

items.title: The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Electronic Monitoring Requirements (Responsible Officer) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Electronic Monitoring Requirements (Responsible Officer) Order (Northern Ireland) 2009 (SR (NI 2009/83) which lists the persons that shall be made responsible for the monitoring of subjects who have an electronic monitoring requirement imposed under Article 35 or 30 of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (S.I. 2008 / 1216 (N.I. 1)).

items.title: The Quality of Bathing Water (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations replace Schedule 1 of the Quality of Bathing Water Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008, to add Brompton Bay, Cushendall, Drain’s Bay, Donaghadee, Portmuck, Rea’s Wood (Inland) and Warrenpoint to the list of identified bathing waters in Regulations.

items.title: The Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) (Independent Guardian) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) (Independent Guardian) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 to remove the requirement for five years’ post qualifying social work experience in order for a person to be eligible for appointment as an independent guardian. The regulations introduce a new eligibility requirement that the person must have completed their post-qualification period of assessed employment.

items.title: The Period Products (Department for Communities Specified Public Service Bodies) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations specify the public service bodies within the functions of the Department for Communities that are subject to the duty to ensure that period products are obtainable free of charge by persons on their premises who need to use them.

items.title: The Kensington Avenue, Banbridge (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Rathenraw Industrial Estate, Antrim (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Period Products (Department of Health Specified Public Service Bodies) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations specify those public service bodies, falling within the functions of the Department of Health, that must ensure that period products are obtainable free of charge by persons in their premises who need to use them.

items.title: The Freight Containers (Safety Convention) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Freight Containers (Safety Convention) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 2) (“the 1992 Regulations”). These Regulations and Guidance implement the UK obligations under the International Convention for Safe Containers, 1972, as amended by resolutions MSC.310(88) and resolution MSC.355(92) (“the Convention”).

items.title: The Rate Relief (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: Regulation 2 tidies drafting of the Rate Relief (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2023 to remove a duplicate amendment within those regulations.

items.title: The Farming with Nature Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2025
items.description: These Regulations establish amendments to the Rural Development Regulation to allow the introduction of the Farming for Nature package by the Department.

items.title: The Road Races (Drumhorc Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places on Roads (Coaches) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Road Races (Cairncastle Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2025
The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring provisions of the Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) into force. Regulation 2 brings section 21 of the Act into force on 4 February 2025. Regulation 3 brings sections 10, 11, 13, 23, 24 and 25 and the schedule of the Act into force on 1 April 2025.

items.title: The Social Security (Up-rating) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for an increase in various values and amounts of forms of assistance payable by virtue of Regulations made under the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 and make amendments to the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations 1976, and the Welfare Foods (Best Start Foods) (Scotland) Regulations 2019.

items.title: The Coronavirus (Recovery and Reform) (Scotland) Act 2022 (Saving Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make saving provision in connection with the expiry of the extensions to criminal procedure time limits in solemn proceedings contained in paragraphs 20 and 22 of the schedule of the Coronavirus (Recovery and Reform) (Scotland) Act 2022. Those time limit extensions will expire at the end of 30 November 2025, in accordance with section 52(1) of that Act.

items.title: The Social Security Up-rating (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order provides for an increase in the various rates of carer’s allowance, attendance allowance, disability living allowance, industrial injuries disablement benefit, industrial death benefit, severe disablement allowance, and personal independence payment. Articles 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 and 8 come into force on 7 April 2025 and Article 5 comes into force on 10 April 2025. Article 1 comes into force on 1 April 2025. Articles 4 and 6 set out the dates on which certain increases take effect.

items.title: The Registers of Scotland (Fees and Plain Copies) Miscellaneous Amendments Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends both the Registers of Scotland (Fees) Order 2014 (“the Fees Order”) and the Registers of Scotland (Information and Access) Order 2014 (“the Information Order”) to provide for:

items.title: The Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Moveable Transactions (Scotland) Act 2023 (asp 3) (“the 2023 Act”). They make modifications and minor corrections for the purposes of, in connection with and for giving effect to the 2023 Act.

items.title: The Community Care (Personal Care and Nursing Care) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Community Care and Health (Scotland) Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”) which, together with the Community Care (Personal Care and Nursing Care) (Scotland) Regulations 2002 (“the principal Regulations”), provides that local authorities are not to charge for certain types of social care provided or secured by them.

items.title: The Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force provisions of the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (“the Act”) on three different dates. The provisions listed in Part 1 of the schedule are brought into force on 14 April 2025. The provisions listed in Part 2 of the schedule are brought into force on 1 August 2025. The provisions listed in Part 3 of the schedule are brought into force on 1 October 2025.

items.title: The Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations commence certain provisions of the Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Disease Control (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends three Orders which make provision relevant to the control of diseases of animals and poultry. The Avian Influenza and Influenza of Avian Origin in Mammals (Scotland) Order 2006 (“the 2006 Order”) is amended to make further provision in relation to measures to deal with any suspected or confirmed outbreak of influenza virus of avian origin in kept mammals. Minor amendments are also made to the Control of Salmonella in Poultry (Breeding, Laying and Broiler Flocks) (Scotland) Order 2009 and the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Order 2002.

items.title: The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Girvan to Lendalfoot) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Electronic Monitoring (Use of Devices and Information) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: The Management of Offenders (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the 2019 Act”) makes provision allowing the courts when making certain disposals to require a person to submit to electronic monitoring of their compliance with a condition of their disposal. The Scottish Ministers can do the same for an individual being released from custody on licence. Section 8 of the 2019 Act gives the Scottish Ministers the power by regulations to prescibe “approved devices” which can be used for electronic monitoring. The types of electronic devices which are approved devices are prescribed in the Electronic Monitoring (Approved Devices) (Scotland) Regulations 2020. Section 9 of the 2019 Act gives the Scottish Ministers the power to make provision about how approved devices may be used and how information obtained through that use may be used. These Regulations do that.

items.title: The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Girvan to Lendalfoot) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2025
The A77 Trunk Road (Doonholm) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (S.S.I. 2023/242) to correct minor errors in the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023. Regulation 2(13) makes provision for instances in which a remedy member’s legacy scheme is the 2007 scheme. This aligns with the provision already set out in regulation 51 of the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 in relation to members whose legacy scheme is the 1992 scheme.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”). They correct minor errors in the 2023 Regulations. Regulation 10 makes new provision for a member who would have been able to transfer in service to the scheme established by the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 or the Police Pensions (Scotland) Regulations 2007 during the relevant period. Such service may now be transferred in provided certain conditions are met.

items.title: The Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force various provisions of the Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The Social Security (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 and Saving and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring sections 2, 9, 17, 20, 21, 22, and 23 of the Social Security (Scotland) (Amendment) Act 2025 (“the Act”) into force on 10 May 2025.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends schedule 1 of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2013, which by virtue of article 3(1)(a) of that Order contains a list of proceedings in relation to which the application of section 4(1) of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 is excluded. The effect is that evidence of previous convictions which have become “spent” under that Act is nonetheless admissible in those proceedings. This Order adds to the list in schedule 1 proceedings relating to sexual harm prevention orders and sexual risk orders under the Abusive Behaviour and Sexual Harm (Scotland) Act 2016.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 4) Order 2025
The Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations correct minor errors in the Teachers’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (S.S.I. 2023/241).

items.title: The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Thurso and Scrabster) (20mph, 30mph and 40mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Golspie) (20mph, 30mph and 40mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of fees to the Scottish Ministers for appeals made under the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 and by virtue of the Town and Country Planning (Control of Advertisements) (Scotland) Regulations 1984. These Regulations apply to the appeals listed in regulation 1(2) The level of fee is to be calculated in accordance with the schedule.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Fees Regulations”) and the Town and Country Planning (Hazardous Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 2015.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Local Reviews) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of fees to a planning authority in respect of local reviews conducted by virtue of section 43A(8)(a) or (b) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997. The level of fee is to be calculated in accordance with the schedule.

items.title: The A87 Trunk Road (Portree) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The A87 Trunk Road (Broadford) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The M9/A9 Trunk Road (Helmsdale) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The A95 Granish – Keith Trunk Road (High Street, Aberlour) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A82 Trunk Road (Fort William) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The B979 Trunk Road (Stonehaven) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The M74 Trunk Road (Junction 9 to Junction 11) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Education (Fees and Student Support) (Miscellaneous Amendment and Revocation) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Fees Regulations”) and the Student Support (Scotland) Regulations 2022 (“the Student Support Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Local Services Franchises Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These regulations make provision about the registration and the variation or cancellation of registrations of services during the transition of a local bus market into, and out of, a franchising framework made by a local transport authority under the Transport (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”). They make provision about registrations, and variations and cancellations of registrations when a franchising framework has been made but before sections 6 to 9 of the Transport Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) cease to have effect in relation to services included in the franchising framework. They also make provision which enables services which could not otherwise be registered to be registered, varied or cancelled, and provide that the relevant notice period in relation to such services expires when a service is no longer included in the franchising framework.

items.title: The Human Tissue (Supply of Information about Transplants) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations specify information that relevant clinicians must provide to the Human Tissue Authority in connection with organ transplants.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints the judges who may hear appeals under section 7 of the Valuation of Lands (Scotland) Amendment Act 1879. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2023 which previously made such provision.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints three judges to hear registration appeals under section 57 of the Representation of the People Act 1983. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2021 which previously made such provision.

items.title: The Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002 Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends Part 2 of schedule 2 of the Scottish Public Services Ombudsman Act 2002. Schedule 2 of that Act specifies the persons liable to investigation by the Scottish Public Services Ombudsman. The Order adds Quality Meat Scotland and the Scottish Pubs Code Adjudicator to the list of persons specified for this purpose. The Order also removes five bodies, which no longer exist, from the schedule.

items.title: The Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend existing provisions regarding the sharing of information in connection with the social security functions of the Scottish Ministers that are contained in the Social Security Information-sharing (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Appeal Committee Procedures) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Appeal Committee Procedures) (Scotland) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”).

items.title: The Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (Consequential Amendments and Forms) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments and prescribe forms in consequence of provision made by the Bankruptcy and Diligence (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”).

items.title: The Valuation (Proposals Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the making of proposals by proprietors, tenants and occupiers of lands and heritages for alteration of entries in the valuation roll for non-domestic rates.

items.title: The Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the Principal Regulations”) which make provision as to the membership and procedure of Health Boards established under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the 1978 Act”). Appointments to the Health Boards are made by the Scottish Ministers.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Highland Council) Designation Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Highland Council) Designation Order 2016 (“the 2016 Order”).

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 to provide for new member contribution rates, with effect from 1 July 2025.

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 3) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”). These Regulations come into force on 1 August 2025.

items.title: The Teachers’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Amendment) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Teachers’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2014, which set out the Scottish Teachers’ Pension Scheme 2015, to clarify and add to those categories of eligible employment for the purposes of that scheme which are specified in schedule 1 of those regulations. In particular, music instructors, educational psychologists, quality improvement managers, and certain other employments relating to educational research and the management of teachers, are added to the categories of eligible employment.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Crieff) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Seed (Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seed (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (“the Principal Regulations”), which prescribe fees for matters relating to:

items.title: The Marketing of Fruit Plant and Propagating Material (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These regulations amend schedules 6, 6A and 7 of the Marketing of Fruit Plant and Propagating Material (Scotland) Regulations 2017 (‘the 2017 Regulations’), which specify Regulated Non-Quarantine Pests (‘RNQPs’) and associated requirements regarding their plant hosts. RNQPs are pests that are present in Scotland and regulated in relation to their plant hosts.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Children (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 3 and Saving Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: The Children (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Act”) received Royal Assent on 1 October 2020. Sections 32, 33 and 35 came into force the following day.

items.title: The Land and Buildings Transaction Tax (Group Relief and Sub-sale Development Relief Modifications) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order modifies the Land and Buildings Transaction Tax (Scotland) Act 2013. It modifies paragraph 7 of schedule 10 and paragraph 4 of schedule 10A which provide certain reliefs from Land and Buildings Transaction Tax.

items.title: The M90/A90 Trunk Road (Junction 10 Craigend Interchange Southbound) (Temporary Vehicle Weight Restriction) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The A835 Trunk Road (Ardcharnich) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2025
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 5) Order 2025
The Environmental Authorisations (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Environmental Authorisations (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (the “2018 Regulations”) to bring water, waste and industrial emissions activities into the framework for authorisation of environmental activities provided by the 2018 Regulations, which currently only apply to radioactive substances activities in Scotland.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Appeals) (Scotland) Regulations 2025 (“the principal instrument”).

items.title: The Agriculture and Rural Communities (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 16 June 2025 the substantive provisions of the Agriculture and Rural Communities (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”) so far as not already in force.

items.title: The Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (Commencement No. 2, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring various provisions of the Charities (Regulation and Administration) (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the Act”) into force. The Bill for the Act received Royal Assent on 9 August 2023. By virtue of section 21(1), that section, together with sections 20 and 22, came into force on 10 August 2023.

items.title: The Prisoners (Early Release) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 11 February 2025 all of the provisions of the Prisoners (Early Release) (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the 2025 Act”), so far as not already in force.

items.title: The Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring provisions of the Circular Economy (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) into force.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) (Amendment) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2025 (“2025 instrument”), which is not yet in force. Paragraph 2(2) corrects a typographical error in paragraph 2(3)(f) of the 2025 instrument. Paragraph 2(3) corrects an error in the new rule 62.28(3AAA) of the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443) which is to be inserted by the 2025 instrument. This instrument substitutes a new rule 62.28(3AAA) for the one to be inserted by the 2025 instrument to correct the error.

The Police (Ethics, Conduct and Scrutiny) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 25 June 2025 sections 1 to 9, 12, 13, 16, 19 and 20 of the Police (Ethics, Conduct and Scrutiny) (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”). Section 9 of the Act, which inserts section 59A (Scottish police advisory list and Scottish police barred list) into the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012, is commenced only for the purpose of making regulations under section 59A(6) and (7).

items.title: The Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2019 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for the control and eradication of bovine viral diarrhoea (“BVD”) in Scotland. The amendments made by article 2 of the Order come into force on 1 July 2025, and the amendments made by article 3 of the Order come into force on 1 February 2026.

items.title: The Regional Strategic Bodies and Regional Colleges (Glasgow and Lanarkshire) Order 2025
items.description: This Order abolishes two regional strategic bodies and designates four regional colleges under the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”).

The Vulnerable Witnesses (Criminal Evidence) (Scotland) Act 2019 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 27 June 2025 sections 6 and 10(4) of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Criminal Evidence) (Scotland) Act 2019 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Scottish Road Works Register (Prescribed Fees) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 112A(1) of the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (“the Act”) provides for the Scottish Road Works Commissioner (“the Commissioner”) to keep a register to be known as the Scottish Road Works Register (“the SRWR”).

The Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) Order 2007. It updates the marine area for which local authorities are designated as planning authority in respect of marine fish farm development so that these areas extend to the extent of the Scottish marine area.

items.title: The Restitution Fund (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: Where a restitution order is imposed on an individual under section 253A of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (“the 1995 Act”), all monies received by the court in payment of the order are to be paid to the Scottish Ministers under section 253B(1). The Scottish Ministers are then obliged under section 253B(2) to pay all monies so received into a fund to be known as the Restitution Fund (“the Fund”). The Restitution Fund (Scotland) Order 2021 (“the 2021 Order”) provided for the delegation of the establishment, maintenance and administration of the Fund to the Scottish Police Benevolent Fund.

items.description: These Regulations correct minor errors in the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”), including in respect of some amendments to the 2023 Regulations made by the Firefighters’ Pensions (Remediable Service) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025.

items.title: The Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Regulations 2020 (“the 2020 Regulations”) which make provision for the operation of a deposit and return scheme. These Regulations come into force on the day after they are made.

items.title: The Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland (Designation of Scheme Administrator) Order 2025
items.description: This Order designates the scheme administrator which will operate and manage the deposit and return scheme (“the Deposit and Return Scheme”) established under the Deposit and Return Scheme for Scotland Regulations 2020. The Deposit and Return Scheme operates in relation to scheme articles. Scheme articles are drinks that are intended to be sold to consumers in Scotland and are contained in single-use packaging made from polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic, aluminium and steel. The Deposit and Return Scheme also applies to scheme packaging, being the packaging for those scheme articles. The Order comes into force on the day after it is made.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Order 2005 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 4) Regulations 2025
items.description: Regulation 2 and the schedule of these Regulations bring into force the provisions of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) specified in column 1 of the table in the schedule on 3 March 2025. If a purpose is stated in column 3 of the table in the schedule, the provision is only brought into force for that purpose. These provisions relate to the register of accredited bodies and registration in that register and will enable applications under section 47(1)(a) of the Disclosure Act to be made and processed by the Scottish Ministers from 3 March 2025.
