The Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the amount of NHS charges which can be recovered from a person who pays compensation to an injured person where that injured person has received National Health Service treatment or ambulance services.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Offshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order prohibits specified methods of fishing within specified areas.

items.title: The Plant Health (Export Certification) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order is made under sections 3(1) and 4A of the Plant Health Act 1967 and makes provision in the area of plant health fees.

items.title: The Home Detention Curfew (Amendment of Specified Time Periods) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: Section 3AA(1) of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) provides Scottish Ministers with the power to release a prisoner on licence. Such a release requires to be subject to a curfew condition (and is commonly known as a release on home detention curfew) (section 12AA of the 1993 Act). Section 3AA(2) provides that Scottish Ministers cannot exercise that power before the prisoner has served one quarter of their sentence. Subsection (3) provides that a short-term prisoner may be released on licence at any point in the period which begins 180 days (i.e. 166 days plus 14 days) before they are otherwise entitled to be released under Part 1 of that Act and ends 14 days before that point is reached. A long-term prisoner may be released on licence during the period which begins 180 days before they have served one-half of their sentence and ends 14 days before that point is reached.

items.title: The Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) on 16 September 2025. With the exception of section 61, the relevant provisions being commenced on that date contain Ministerial powers to make subordinate legislation. Section 61 (Regulations) specifies the Parliamentary procedure applicable to regulations made under certain provisions of the Act.

items.title: The National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“2024 Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the common staffing method. The common staffing method was introduced into the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“1978 Act”) by section 4 of the Health and Care (Staffing) (Scotland) Act 2019 and includes, among other things, the use of a “staffing level tool” and a “professional judgement tool” in determining the appropriate staffing levels for particular kinds of health care provision.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the Principal Regulations”) which regulate the system of rally control on public roads in Scotland. The Regulations provide a new fee structure for rallies and make minor amendments to update various references.

items.title: The Caledonian Maritime Assets (Port Ellen Works) Harbour Revision Order 2025
items.description: This Order, made on the application of the Caledonian Maritime Assets Limited, empowers CMAL to construct, maintain and operate in the Isle of Islay and on land at Port Ellen, harbour works and harbour related facilities at the harbour.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 2) Order 2025

The Council Tax (Dwellings and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Council Tax (Dwellings and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Fees for Scheme Membership and Disclosure Applications (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision under sections 70(1) and (2) of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”) for the fees payable in respect of applications to join the PVG Scheme and for confirmation of scheme membership. They also make provision under section 61(1), (2)(a) and (b) and (3) of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) in respect of applications for Level 1 and Level 2 disclosures.

items.title: The Social Security (Residence and Presence Requirements) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the social security assistance entitlement of certain persons arriving in Scotland from Israel, the Occupied Palestinian Territories (the West Bank, including East Jerusalem, and Gaza) or the occupied Golan Heights following the escalation of violence between Israel and Iran that began on 13 June 2025. These Regulations also make provision for persons who have been granted, or are deemed to have been granted, leave outside the immigration rules made under section 3(2) of the Immigration Act 1971.

items.title: The Redemption of Heritable Securities (Excluded Securities) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order provides that section 11(2) of the Land Tenure Reform (Scotland) Act 1974 will not apply to the heritable securities granted in pursuance of the schemes mentioned in article 2.

The National Health Service Superannuation and Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”), the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (2008 Section) (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”), the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”), and the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Transitional and Consequential Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the Transitional Regulations”).

items.title: The Level 1 and Level 2 Disclosure Review Application (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with the procedure for the carrying out of reviews (whether by the Scottish Minsters, the chief constable or the independent reviewer) under Part 1 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) in relation to any of the information included in a Level 1 or Level 2 disclosure.

items.title: The Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 Amendment and Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025 so that provisions in Part 8 (information to be included with certain electronic material at Scottish elections) of the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (“the Act”) will not come into force on 1 October 2025. Instead those provisions, which are listed in Part 1 of the schedule, will come into force on 17 November 2025.

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Falls of Falloch) (Clearway) Order 2025
The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 2014 (“the 2014 Order”). The 2014 Order designates sports grounds, classes of sporting events played at those grounds and classes of sporting events outside Great Britain for the purposes of Part II of the Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (sporting events: control of alcohol etc.).

items.title: The Representation of the People (Absent Voting at Local Government Elections) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Representation of the People (Absent Voting at Local Government Elections) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”). The 2007 Regulations apply where a postal or proxy vote is being or has been sought in respect solely of local government elections in Scotland or of a particular local government election in Scotland.

The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2025

The Rural Support (Simplification and Improvement) (Data Publication) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations simplify and improve the operation of CAP legislation by amending the requirements for publication of the CAP beneficiaries by no longer requiring this to be done on a single UK website.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 5) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Scottish Water, Prevention of Water Pollution (Milngavie Waterworks) Byelaws Extension Order 2025
items.description:
This Order extends the period during which the Scottish Water, Prevention of Water Pollution (Milngavie Waterworks) Byelaws 2015 are to remain in force to 29 September 2026. A copy of the Byelaws may be obtained from Scottish Water, The Bridge, Buchanan Gate Business Park, Cumbernauld Road, Stepps, G33 6FB, and online at 
www.scottishwater.co.uk.

items.title: The Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Amendment Rules 2025
items.description: These Rules amend the Prisons and Young Offenders Institutions (Scotland) Rules 2011 (“the principal Rules”).

items.title: The Qualifications Scotland (Appointment of Initial Members) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide that the persons mentioned in the schedule who are, immediately before the member appointment date, members of the Scottish Qualifications Authority, are to be taken to have been appointed as members of Qualifications Scotland under paragraph 2(1) of schedule 1 of the Education (Scotland) Act 2025.

items.title: The Cromarty Firth Port Authority Harbour Revision Order 2025
items.description: This Order defines the harbour limits and confers updated powers on the Cromarty Firth Port Authority, including powers to lease, the making of byelaws and issuing of general and special directions. It also amends or repeals obsolete or otherwise unnecessary statutory provision affecting the port.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Injurious Articles) (Fixed Penalty Notices and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for a fixed penalty notice procedure in relation to offences under the Environmental Protection (Microbeads) (Scotland) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”), the Environmental Protection (Cotton Buds) (Scotland) Regulations 2019 (“the 2019 Regulations”), the Environmental Protection (Single-use Plastic Products) (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”), and the Environmental Protection (Single-use Vapes) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the making and recovery of charges for health services provided to certain persons who are not ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (Scottish Carbon Budgets) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made under section A4(1) of the Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”) which requires the Scottish Ministers to set Scottish carbon budgets so that every year between 2026 and the net-zero emissions target year is covered by a budget. A Scottish carbon budget is the number of tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent prescribed for a given period. The Scottish Ministers have a duty under section A3 of the 2009 Act to ensure that each Scottish carbon budget target is met.

items.title: The Winter Heating Assistance (Pension Age) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Winter Heating Assistance (Pension Age) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (‘the principal Regulations’).

items.title: The Climate Change (Local Development Plan) (Repeals) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order repeals section 3F of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”) and sections 72 and 73 of the Climate Change (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”).

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification) Order 2025
items.description: This Order specifies the office holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Scottish Parliament. Part 1 of the schedule specifies the office-holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Parliament. Part 2 of the schedule specifies the office-holders who are disqualified from being a member of the Parliament but only for particular constituencies or regions.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Constituencies and Regions) Order 2025
items.description:
This Order gives effect to the recommendations contained in the Report on the Second Review of Scottish Parliament Boundaries by Boundaries Scotland dated April 2025. The report is available on the Boundaries Scotland website (https://boundaries.scot/) and hard copies can also be requested by telephoning 0131 244 2001 or by emailing 
boundaries.scot@scottishboundaries.gov.uk.

items.title: The A78 Trunk Road (Greenock) (Temporary Prohibition of Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Scottish Parliament Elections (Regional Returning Officers and Constituency Returning Officers) Order 2025
items.description: This Order designates regional returning officers for the purposes of elections to the Scottish Parliament. It also directs the constituency returning officers for those purposes for constituencies situated in more than one local authority area. For constituencies wholly situated in one local authority area, the constituency returning officer is the person who is the returning officer for elections of councillors for that local authority.

items.title: The Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000 (Non-Party Campaigner Code of Practice) (Appointed Date) (Scotland) Order 2025 (revoked)
items.description: A Code of Practice on controlled expenditures made by non-party campaigners during the Scottish devolved regulated period, which precedes a Scottish Parliament election, has been prepared by the Electoral Commission in accordance with section 100AA(1) of the Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000.

items.title: The Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025 (Statutory Guidance on Imprints on Electronic Materials) (Appointed Date) Regulations 2025
items.description: Statutory guidance on information to be included with electronic materials at Scottish elections has been prepared by the Electoral Commission in accordance with section 58(1) of the Scottish Elections (Representation and Reform) Act 2025. This guidance concerns the operation of the duties on campaigners under Part 8 of that Act (which requires “imprint” information to be included with electronic campaign material) and the exercise of the Electoral Commission’s functions in relation to breaches or suspected breaches of those duties.

items.title: The Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Saving Provision) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 10, and schedule 3, of the Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”) will expire at the end of 31 March 2025 by virtue of section 11 of the 2022 Act read alongside the Cost of Living (Tenant Protection) (Scotland) Act 2022 (Expiry of Section 10: Extension) Regulations 2024. The Rent Adjudication (Temporary Modifications) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the 2024 Regulations”) were made under powers created by schedule 3 of the 2022 Act and will therefore cease to have effect at the end of 31 March 2025.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Lochgilphead) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 4) Regulations 2025 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations bring section 6 (effect of plan) of the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the Act”) into force on 16 December 2025.

items.title: The A7 Trunk Road (Langholm) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2025
The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 3) Order 2025
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2025
The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the list of prescribed rural housing bodies in the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Order 2004 to include Tiree Community Development Trust and Woodhaven Housing Company Limited. Rural housing bodies are bodies in favour of which it is competent to create a rural housing burden comprising a right of pre-emption under section 43(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Public Procurement (Agreement on Government Procurement) (Thresholds) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the thresholds that apply in the field of public procurement that are specified in the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015, the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 and the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 respectively in accordance with the obligations of the United Kingdom under the Agreement on Government Procurement agreed among certain World Trade Organisation members including the United Kingdom and as required by each set of Regulations being amended.

items.title: The Disability Assistance (Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for Scottish Adult Disability Living Allowance which is a type of disability assistance given by the Scottish Ministers under section 31 of the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018.

items.title: The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the amount which a local authority is to assume that a person will need for their personal requirements per week, in assessing the person’s ability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Competitions and Trials) (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the 1976 Regulations”) which make provision about the authorisation of motor vehicle competitions and trials, other than trials of speed, on public roads in Scotland.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Intimation and Service) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision so that a requirement in any Act of Sederunt or in the Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 that a document be placed on the walls or doors of civil courts may also be fulfilled by making certain information publicly available on the Scottish Courts and Tribunals Service website.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Electronic Signature and Electronic Transmission of Documents) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for the electronic signature and transmission of documents in civil court proceedings in Scotland.

items.title: The Food (Promotion and Placement) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for restrictions on the promotion and placement of certain foods and drinks that are less healthy.

items.title: The Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 (Commencement) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force on 31 March 2026 Part 2 of schedule 8 and section 179 (so far as it relates to that part) of the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Tay Crossing to Ballinluig) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Specific Turns, Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Councillors) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a councillor on a Scottish council is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after the day on which they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Members of the House of Commons) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a member of the House of Commons (an “MP”) is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after the day on which they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Disqualification of Members of the House of Lords) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scotland Act 1998 to provide that a person who is a member of the House of Lords (“a HoL member”) is disqualified from membership of the Scottish Parliament, subject to the exceptions provided. These Regulations come into force on the day after they are made, but do not disqualify any member of the Scottish Parliament (an “MSP”) until the day of the poll at the next general election for members of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The A737 Trunk Road (Dalry) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road and Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”). The 1992 Regulations concern the assessment of a person’s ability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”). By virtue of section 87(3) of the 1968 Act, accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 is to be regarded as accommodation provided under Part III of the National Assistance Act 1948.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The A83 Trunk Road (Tarbert) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Building (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (the “2004 Regulations”) and come into force on 6 April 2026.

items.title: The Scottish Parliament (Elections etc.) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Scottish Parliament (Elections etc.) Order 2015 (“the 2015 Order”). The 2015 Order sets out provisions relating to the conduct of elections for, and the return of members to, the Scottish Parliament.

The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025

The A83 Trunk Road (Tarbert) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Scottish Register of Tartans Fees Order 2025
items.description: This Order updates the fees payable for services provided by the Keeper of the Scottish Register of Tartans and revokes the Scottish Register of Tartans Fees Order 2009.

items.title: The Official Controls (Import of High Risk Food and Feed of Non-Animal Origin) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/1793 on the temporary increase of official controls and emergency measures governing the entry into the Union of certain goods from certain third countries (“Regulation (EU) 2019/1793”).

items.title: The Social Security (Cross-border Provision, Case Transfer and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for various amendments to forms of assistance payable by virtue of regulations made under the Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018 (“the 2018 Act”), to the 2018 Act itself, and to the Welfare Foods (Best Start Foods) (Scotland) Regulations 2019 (“the Best Start Foods Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the 2006 Regulations”) which make provision for general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Free-Range Poultrymeat Marketing Standards (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend Commission Regulation (EC) No 543/2008 (“EUR 2008/543”) which lays down detailed rules as regards marketing standards for poultrymeat.

items.title: The A96 Trunk Road (Nairn) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The A76 Trunk Road (Sanquhar) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Carer’s Assistance (Miscellaneous and Consequential Amendments, Revocation, Transitional and Saving Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Carer’s Assistance (Carer Support Payment) (Scotland) Regulations 2023 (“the principal Regulations”) to make provision for the introduction of Carer Support consisting of three components: Carer Support Payment, Carer Additional Person Payment and Scottish Carer Supplement. These Regulations also make miscellaneous amendments to the Carer’s Assistance (Young Carer Grants) (Scotland) Regulations 2019 and the principal Regulations, including consequential, revocation, transitional and saving provisions.

items.title: The Charities Accounts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charities Accounts (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”). They are made under powers in the Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”).

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Oban) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Early Release of Prisoners (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations are made under the powers contained in section 3C of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”).

items.title: The A78 Trunk Road (Greenock) (Temporary Prohibition of Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 2) Order 2025
The A76 Trunk Road (Kirkconnel) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 2) Order 2025
The A737 Trunk Road (Dalry) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road and Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000 (Non-Party Campaigner Code of Practice) (Appointed Date) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2025
items.description: A Code of Practice on controlled expenditures made by non-party campaigners during the Scottish devolved regulated period, which precedes a Scottish Parliament election, has been prepared by the Electoral Commission in accordance with section 100AA(1) of the Political Parties, Elections and Referendums Act 2000.

items.title: The Scottish Languages Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force sections 1, 2, 7, 9(1), 9(2)(c), 9(6), 10 to 16, 18 to 20, 23, 26, 27, 31, 32(1), 32(5), 32(8), 33, 37 to 39, 41 and 46 of the Scottish Languages Act 2025 (“the Act”) for all purposes on 30 November 2025 (“the appointed date”).

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Ballantrae) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The A78 Trunk Road (Inverkip) (Temporary 40mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Education (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitory Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force various provisions of the Education (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”) on 1 December 2025.

items.title: The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 6, Consequential Amendment, Saving and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: Regulation 2(1) of these Regulations brings into force section 81 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) on 12 January 2026. Section 81 of the Disclosure Act repeals sections 7, 11, 32(1) and (2) and schedule 1 of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”) relating to court references for relevant and other offences.

items.title: The Representation of the People Act 1983 Remedial (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order makes temporary modifications to the law relating to Scottish parliamentary elections and local government elections in Scotland to remove an incompatibility with the European Convention on Human Rights arising from the disfranchisement of some persons detained in hospital under orders and directions relating to criminal justice from voting in Scottish parliamentary elections and local government elections in Scotland. This brings the law into line with the changes to the rules on prisoner voting made by Part 3 of the Scottish Elections (Franchise and Representation) Act 2020.

items.title: The Absent Voting at Scottish Parliament and Local Government Elections (Signature Refresh) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Scottish Parliament (Elections etc.) Order 2015 (“the 2015 Order”) and the Representation of the People (Scotland) Regulations 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2025 (“2025 Act”).

items.title: The Regulation of Care (Child Contact Services) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Public Services Reform (Scotland) Act 2010 (“the Act”) to add “child contact service” as a category of care services regulated by Social Care and Social Work Improvement Scotland (commonly known as the Care Inspectorate).

items.title: The Regulation of Care (Child Contact Services) (Equality) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations confer an additional function upon Social Care and Social Work Improvement Scotland (commonly known as “the Care Inspectorate”) in relation to child contact services.

items.title: The A701 Trunk Road (Dumfries) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2025
The Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Amendment (Amendment) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Amendment Regulations 2025, which in turn amend the Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”).

items.title: The Burial and Cremation (Applications and Registers) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cremation (Scotland) Regulations 2019 (“the Cremation Regulations”) and the Burial (Applications and Register) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“the Burial Applications Regulations”).

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Campbeltown) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 3) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2025
The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The A85 Trunk Road (Oban) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 3) Order 2025
The A82 Trunk Road (Fort William) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Equality Act 2010 (Specification of Public Authorities) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order adds Zero Waste Scotland Limited to the list of Scottish public authorities in Part 3 of schedule 19 of the Equality Act 2010 (“the Act”). The public authorities listed in schedule 19 are subject to the public sector equality duty in section 149 of the Act.

items.title: The Equality Act 2010 (Specific Duties) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Equality Act 2010 (Specific Duties) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order specifies a rate of 49.8 pence in the pound as the non-domestic rate to be levied throughout Scotland in respect of the financial year 2025-2026. A rate of 49.8 pence in the pound was also prescribed by the Scottish Ministers as the non-domestic rate for the financial years 2022-2023 (S.S.I. 2022/36), 2023-2024 (S.S.I. 2023/29), and 2024-2025 (S.S.I. 2024/3).

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Callander) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 3) Order 2025
The A75 Trunk Road (Springholm) (Temporary 20mph and 40mph Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Cairnryan) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The A75/A77 Trunk Road (Stranraer) (Temporary 20mph and 40mph Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Scottish Aggregates Tax (Administration) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the administration and assurance of Scottish aggregates tax. In particular:

items.title: The Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act (Record Keeping) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act (Record Keeping) Regulations 2015 to make provision for the records that must be preserved by registrable persons, and certain parties made exempt from registration, under the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024.

items.title: The Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act (Postponement of Tax Pending a Review or Appeal) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make legislative amendments so that where a review or appeal under Part 11 of the Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act 2014 (“the Act”) is in progress, a taxpayer may make an application to Revenue Scotland to postpone the payment of tax, penalty or interest in relation to either a liability for aggregates tax under the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 or a liability for Scottish landfill tax under the Landfill Tax (Scotland) Act 2014, notwithstanding the provision in section 245 of the Act that in such a situation tax, penalty and interest are due and payable. As provided for in the Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act (Postponement of Tax Pending a Review or Appeal) Regulations 2015, a postponement can be granted if Revenue Scotland considers that there are exceptional circumstances.

items.title: The Social Security (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
The First-tier Tribunal for Scotland (Transfer of Functions and Members of the Police Appeals Tribunal) Regulations 2025
items.description: The Police Appeals Tribunal was set up by the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012 to deal with appeals under section 56(1) of that Act against a decision to dismiss or demote a constable inrank.

items.title: The Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 3) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force various provisions of the Children (Care and Justice) (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying and Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision as regards the amount payable as non-domestic rates in certain circumstances in respect of certain non-domestic properties in Scotland. For the financial year 2025-2026, the non-domestic rate poundage is fixed by the Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2025 (S.S.I. 2025/37).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 2010 (“2010 Regulations”) to allow Health Boards to include the name of a dentist on their dental lists on a provisional basis where that dentist has not yet completed mandatory training where required by regulation 5A, and amend provisions relating to the requirement to obtain the prior approval of the Scottish Dental Practice Board before carrying out care and treatment.

items.title: The A76 Trunk Road (Sanquhar) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Budget (Scotland) Act 2025 Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Budget (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”).

items.title: The First-tier Tribunal for Scotland General Regulatory Chamber Police Appeals and Upper Tribunal for Scotland (Composition) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the composition of the First-tier Tribunal for Scotland when dealing with police appeals in the General Regulatory Chamber. They also make provision as to the composition of the Upper Tribunal for Scotland when hearing police appeals.

items.title: The First-tier Tribunal for Scotland General Regulatory Chamber (Police Appeals) (Procedure) Regulations 2025
items.description: The Tribunals (Scotland) Act 2014 (“the 2014 Act”) created a new structure for tribunals dealing with devolved matters under the judicial leadership of the Lord President of the Court of Session as head of the Scottish Tribunals.

items.title: The Education (Scotland) Act 2025 (Consequential Provisions) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in consequence of the Education (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”). The Act establishes Qualifications Scotland. This replaces the Scottish Qualifications Authority (“the SQA”) which is dissolved by the Act.

items.title: The Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 3) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force sections 54, 55, 56, 57 and 58 of the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”) on 19 January 2026.

items.title: The Common Services Agency (Membership and Procedure) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Common Services Agency (Membership and Procedure) Regulations 1991 (“the 1991 Regulations”) which make provision with respect to the Common Services Agency for the Scottish Health Service (“the Agency”).

items.title: The Care Reform (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring various provisions of the Care Reform (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”) into force. The Bill for the Act received Royal Assent on 22 July 2025. By virtue of section 39(1) of the Act, both section 30 and the entirety of Part 3 of the Act came into force on the following day.

items.title: The A75 Trunk Road (Crocketford) (Temporary 20mph and 40mph Speed Restrictions) Order 2025
The Non-Domestic Rates (Transitional Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in Scotland. They apply to the financial year 2025-2026. The Regulations do not apply where a non-domestic property is shown in a merged, split or reorganised entry in the valuation roll which takes effect during the financial year 2025-2026.

items.title: The Conservation of Salmon (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Conservation of Salmon (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (“the 2016 Regulations”), which make provision for regulating the killing of salmon in specified areas.

items.title: The Public Procurement (Iraq Free Trade Agreement) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015, the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 and the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (“Scottish public procurement regulations”).

items.title: The Cross-Border Public Procurement (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for how devolved Scottish procurement legislation applies to cross-border procurement following the enactment of the Procurement Act 2023 (“the 2023 Act”). The Regulations apply devolved Scottish procurement legislation to UK contracting authorities regulated by the 2023 Act in certain circumstances. They also disapply devolved Scottish procurement legislation where the 2023 Act regulates cross-border procurement by a devolved Scottish authority (as defined by that Act). The provisions applied or disapplied are listed in tables inserted as schedules to the relevant devolved Scottish procurement legislation. In some cases, provisions are applied with the modifications specified in those tables.

items.title: The Victims, Witnesses, and Justice Reform (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force a number of sections of the Victims, Witnesses, and Justice Reform (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Scottish Parliament Elections (Returning Officer Fees and Charges) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for the total amounts that are recoverable by returning officers for their services and expenses in connection with the conduct of elections for membership of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 4) Revocation Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 4) Regulations 2025 which would have brought into force section 6 of the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the Act”) on 16 December 2025. As a result section 6 will no longer come into force on 16 December 2025.

items.title: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Notification Requirements) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Notification Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 to add new notification requirements relating to gender recognition. Where an offender who is subject to the notification requirements in Part 2 of the Sexual Offences Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) makes an initial notification to the police under section 83 of that Act, this will now be required to include, where applicable, the information that the offender has made an application for a gender recognition certificate which has not yet been determined or has obtained a full gender recognition certificate which was issued on or after the “relevant date”. “Relevant date” has the same meaning as in section 82(6) of the 2003 Act (and is usually the date of conviction). Where an offender subject to the notification requirements in Part 2 of the 2003 Act makes an application for a gender recognition certificate or is issued with a full gender recognition certificate after making their initial notification to the police, the offender will be required to notify this as a change of circumstances under section 84 of the 2003 Act.

items.title: The Rural Support (Improvement) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make modifications to improve the operation of the provisions of Regulation (EU) No1307/2013 (the “Direct Payments Regulation”) and the Common Agricultural Policy (Direct Payments etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (the “2015 Regulations”) in relation to the requirements for ecological focus areas and the terms of the Scottish Suckler Beef Support Scheme.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 3) Order 2025
The A702 Trunk Road (Biggar) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Prescribed Services and Activities) (Protected Adult) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 94 (as amended by section 75(2) of the Disclosure Act) of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”) defines who a “protected adult” is for the purposes of the PVG Act. Under that section, the receipt by an individual of a prescribed health service or a prescribed community care service confers on them the status of a ‘protected adult’. Section 97(1) of the PVG Act provides that “prescribed” means prescribed in Regulations made by the Scottish Minsters.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Hospitality Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision for relief from liability to pay non-domestic rates for lands and heritages used for certain purposes, for the financial year 2025-2026.

items.title: The Domestic Abuse (Protection) (Scotland) Act 2021 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force, on 1 August 2026, sections 2, 3 and 22 of the Domestic Abuse (Protection) (Scotland) Act 2021 (“the Act”). Sections 2 and 3 are commenced for the purposes of Part 2 of the Act. Section 2 of the Act provides a meaning of “abusive behaviour” for the purposes of Part 1 of the Act, and section 3 provides additional clarification as to what behaviour constitutes “abusive behaviour”. The description in section 3 is non-exhaustive.

items.title: The Sheep Carcase (Classification and Price Reporting) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations provide for the mandatory classification and price reporting of sheep carcases by approved slaughterhouses. These Regulations come into force on 12 January 2026, except for the provisions specified in regulation 1(3) relating to automated classification methods, which come into force on 12 February 2027.

items.title: The Removing from Heritable Property (Form of Charge) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Removing from Heritable Property (Form of Charge) (Scotland) Regulations 2011 which prescribe the Form of Charge required to be served by an officer of the court before removing a person, and any effects that person may have, from heritable property by virtue of a court decree or warrant.

items.title: The Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Decrees for Removing from Heritable Property) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: Part 15 of the Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) sets out the general procedures which are to be followed when removing a person from heritable property. Section 214(2) of the 2007 Act contains a list of the decrees, orders and warrants which are covered by the general procedures.

items.title: The Scottish Secure Tenancies (Proceedings for Possession) (Form of Notice) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scottish Secure Tenancies (Proceedings for Possession) (Form of Notice) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”). The 2012 Regulations prescribe the form of notice to be used by a landlord when notifying its tenant and any qualifying occupier under a Scottish secure tenancy, within the meaning of Part 2 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”), that the landlord may raise proceedings for recovery of possession of the house.

items.title: The Public Bodies (Joint Working) (Integration Joint Boards) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Public Bodies (Joint Working) (Integration Joint Boards) (Scotland) Order 2014 (the 2014 Order). Article 2(2) provides that integration joint board members, appointed under article 3(6) of the 2014 Order to represent third sector bodies, service users and unpaid carers, can vote at board meetings. Article 2(3) and (4) make corresponding amendments relating to the resignation of members and proxy voting.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025 (“the Order”) amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/199) (“the Scheme”). The schedule of the Order makes various amendments to the Scheme relating to retained firefighters. These include amendments to introduce new awards which are payable following the death of a retained firefighter, to extend the deadline for their purchase of service during the extended limited period and to amend the eligibility criteria for some awards and conversions.

items.title: The Retained EU Law (Revocation and Reform) Act 2023 (Agricultural Products) (Consequential Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to domestic legislation in consequence of the renaming of retained EU law (and related terms) as assimilated law (and related terms), as set out in section 5(1) of the Retained EU Law (Revocation and Reform) Act 2023.

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2025 (“the 2025 Act”).

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Brora) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Scottish Landfill Tax (Standard Rate and Lower Rate) Order 2025
items.description: This Order specifies the standard rate and lower rate for Scottish landfill tax for disposals on or after 1 April 2025. The standard rate is £126.15 and the lower rate is £4.05. Tax is to be calculated by reference to the weight and types of material disposed of, as provided for in section 13 of the Landfill Tax (Scotland) Act 2014.

items.title: The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Girvan) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A702 Trunk Road (Biggar) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2025
The Social Security (Residence in an EEA State or Switzerland) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Invalid Care Allowance) Regulations 1976, the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 and the Social Security (Personal Independence Payment) Regulations 2013. They reflect that claimants, to whom a relevant EU regulation applied on 31st December 2020 (the date of the end of the transition period following the UK’s withdrawal from the EU), maintain their rights to carer’s allowance, the care component of disability living allowance and the daily living component of personal independence payment subject to certain conditions. The individual must have continuously received these benefits since 31st December 2020, and they must not have been habitually resident in the United Kingdom on or after that date. These Regulations do not create a ground for new claims but provide a legislative basis to continue paying benefits already in payment.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2025
The Energy Performance of Buildings (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision under the sections 1(1) and 49(1) of the UK Withdrawal from the European Union (Continuity) (Scotland) Act 2021 and sections 250 and 331(2) of the Energy Act 2023. They come fully into force on 31 October 2026 and are partially in force on 1 January 2026 so as to enable the approval of organisations and of accreditation schemes under regulations 11 and 12 respectively.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (S.S.I. 2000/95) (“the 2000 Regulations”) to correct errors in the 2000 Regulations and in certain amendments made to those the 2000 Regulations by the Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2020 (“the 2020 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Common Staffing Method) (Scotland) Regulations 2024 (“2024 Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the common staffing method. The common staffing method was introduced into the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“1978 Act”) by section 4 of the Health and Care (Staffing) (Scotland) Act 2019 and includes, among other things, the use of a “staffing level tool” and a “professional judgement tool” in determining the appropriate staffing levels for particular kinds of health care provision.

items.title: The Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (Modification) Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 53(2) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) provides that an education authority must provide or secure the provision of a school lunch (as defined in section 53(12)) free of charge to pupils falling within section 53(7).

items.title: The Burial (Management) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision about the management of burial grounds in Scotland.

items.title: The Burial and Cremation (Inspection) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision regarding the carrying out of inspections of burial authorities, cremation authorities and funeral directors (“the relevant bodies”) and their associated premises by inspectors appointed under Part 4 (inspection) of the Burial and Cremation (Scotland) Act 2016 (“the 2016 Act”). The Regulations also make provision in relation to the enforcement of the duties on the relevant bodies specified in regulation 3.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules 1996 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2025
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Criminal Procedure Rules 1996.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) (Amendment) Regulations 2025
items.description: Regulation 61 of the Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2018 provides that an employer exiting the Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) may in certain circumstances be liable to receive an exit credit from the relevant scheme administering authority.

items.title: The Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 2 (Amendment) and Commencement No. 3) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025 and bring into force provisions of the Good Food Nation (Scotland) Act 2022 (“the Act”) on 10 March 2025.

items.title: The Disclosure Information (Accredited Bodies) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make further provision in connection with the register of accredited bodies maintained by the Scottish Ministers under section 46 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) and registration in it.

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2025
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Bail and Release from Custody (Scotland) Act 2023 (Commencement No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force sections 1 (decisions on bail: relevant information), 2 (determination of good reason for refusing bail), 3 (removal of restriction on bail in certain solemn cases), and 4 (decisions on bail: duty to state and record reasons) of the Bail and Release from Custody (Scotland) Act 2023 (“the 2023 Act”). These sections will come into force on 14 May 2025.

items.title: The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Council Tax Reduction and Council Tax (Discounts) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax (Discounts) (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the Discount Regulations”), the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2021 (“the 2021 Regulations”).

items.title: The Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) (Scotland) Order 1997 (“the 1997 Order”), which lists those classes of dwellings which are exempt from council tax in Scotland.

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Spean Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Police Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Rural Support (Improvement) (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make modifications to improve the operation of the provisions of Regulation (EU) No  1306/2013 (the “Horizontal Regulation”), Regulation (EU) No  1307/2013 (the “Direct Payments Regulation”), and Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No  809/2014 (the integrated administration and control regulation or “IACS Regulation”).

items.title: The Building (Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the Fees Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: This Order–

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 Amendment) (Nautical Assessors) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Chapter 12 (assessors) of the Rules of the Court of Session 1994. Paragraph 2(2) amends rule 12.5 to remove the requirement that a list of nautical assessors be kept. Paragraph 2(3) amends rule 12.7 to remove the bar on leading a skilled witness on nautical matters where a nautical assessor is assisting the court.

items.title: The A84/A85 Trunk Road (Callander) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The St. Andrews Harbour Revision Order 2025
items.description: This Order defines the harbour limits and confers updated powers on the Trustees, including powers to lease, the making of byelaws and the issuing of general and special directions.

items.title: The A737 Trunk Road (Dalry Road) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A85 Trunk Road (Crieff) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The Recognition of Overseas Qualifications (Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations set out the charges payable in connection with services regarding the recognition of qualifications provided in pursuance of international obligations.

items.title: The A830 Trunk Road (Mallaig) (20mph Speed Limit) Order 2025
The International Organisations (Immunities and Privileges) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the International Organisations (Immunities and Privileges) (Scotland) Order 2009 (“the 2009 Order”).

items.title: The Little Loch Broom Scallops Several Fishery (Variation) Order 2025
items.description: This Order varies the Little Loch Broom Scallops Several Fishery Order 2015 by—

items.title: The A835/A893 Trunk Road (Contin) (20mph Speed Limit) Order 2025
The A835/A893 Trunk Road (Ullapool) (20mph and 30mph Speed Limits) Order 2025
The A82 Trunk Road (Spean Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2025
The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 5, Saving and Transitional Provision) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 5, Saving and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025 (“the Principal Regulations”) and come into force on 31 March 2025.

items.title: The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Incidental, Supplementary and Consequential Provision) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations make provision in consequence of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”).

items.title: The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (List A and B Offences) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend schedules 1 and 2 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”).

items.title: The Regulated Roles with Children and Adults (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: Section 74 of the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (“the Disclosure Act”) amended section 91 of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the PVG Act”). Paragraphs (3) and (4) of section 74 substitute schedules 2 and 3 of the PVG Act for the schedules contained in schedules 3 and 4 of the Disclosure Act. Section 74 of the Disclosure Act and these Regulations will come into force on the same day.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2013 (“the 2013 Order”).

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (“the 2000 Regulations”) to correct errors in certain amendments made to the 2000 Regulations by the Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2020.

items.title: The Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Aggregates Tax and Devolved Taxes Administration (Scotland) Act 2024 on 1 April 2025. Section 53 makes a minor amendment to the Revenue Scotland and Tax Powers Act 2014. Section 59 makes provision regarding the role of a designated officer of Revenue Scotland. Section 60 implements a provision in relation to Land and Buildings Transaction Tax.

items.title: The Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”). They substitute increased annual amounts for different categories of local authority remuneration to their members. They also provide for the local authorities currently falling under Band A in the schedule to be moved to Band B and for an increase to 10 of the maximum number of Senior Councillors in certain local authorities.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Ordinary Cause Rules 1993 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2025
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Chapter 62 of the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 in consequence of the United Kingdom signing the Hague Convention of 2nd July 2019 on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Judgments on 12th January 2024. Chapter 62 has also been amended to remove spent provisions in Part V.

items.title: The Railway Closure (Exclusion) (Hairmyres) Order 2025
items.description: Section 30 of the Railways Act 2005 provides for a closure procedure where there is a proposal by a funding authority to close a station. Section 38 of that Act enables the exclusion of stations from that procedure and article 2 of this Order excludes the station at Hairmyres, in the local government area of South Lanarkshire, from the procedure. The station at Hairmyres is being relocated as part of the East Kilbride Enhancement Project.

items.title: The Provision of Early Learning and Childcare (Specified Children) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Provision of Early Learning and Childcare (Specified Children) (Scotland) Order 2014 (“the 2014 Order”). The 2014 Order specifies those children who are eligible pre-school children for the purposes of section 47(2)(c)(ii) of the Children and Young People (Scotland) Act 2014 and who are therefore entitled to the mandatory amount of early learning and childcare (as defined in section 48 of that Act).

items.title: The A86 Trunk Road (Kingussie) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 5, Saving and Transitional Provision) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Disclosure (Scotland) Act 2020 (Commencement No. 5, Saving and Transitional Provision) Regulations 2025 (“the Principal Regulations”) and come into force on 31 March 2025.

items.title: The A7 Trunk Road (Langholm) (Prohibition of Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The A7 Trunk Road (Langholm) (Temporary 20mph Speed Restriction) Order 2025
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2025
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2025
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2025
The Sheriff Appeal Court Fees Amendment Order 2025
items.description: This Order amends the Sheriff Appeal Court Fees Order 2024 (“the 2024 Order”) which makes provision for the fees payable in the Sheriff Appeal Court, to the Clerk of the Sheriff Appeal Court, any officer acting for the Clerk, or the auditor of the Sheriff Appeal Court.

items.title: The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2025
The A83 Trunk Road (Campbeltown) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2025
The Tied Pubs (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Tied Pubs (Scotland) Act 2021 (“the Act”) and the Scottish Pubs Code Regulations 2024 (“the Code”) and come into force on 30 March 2025.

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2024 Amendment Regulations 2025
items.description: These Regulations amend the Budget (Scotland) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Fort Augustus) (Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2025
The Public Services Reform (Scotland) Act 2010 (Part 2 Further Extension) Order 2025
items.description: In terms of section 134(3) of the Public Services Reform (Scotland) Act 2010, Part 2 of that Act ceases to have effect 5 years after being brought into force unless the Scottish Ministers make an order extending the period of effect. Part 2 is concerned with order-making powers relating to the public functions of certain bodies and came into force on 1 August 2010.

items.title: The Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003 (Treatment of Qualifications Scotland as Specified Authority) Order 2025
items.description: This Order provides that Qualifications Scotland, for the purpose of or in connection with appointments to that body, is to be treated as if it were a specified authority listed in schedule 2 of the Public Appointments and Public Bodies etc. (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The National Bus Travel Concession Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendment) (Scotland) Order 2025
items.description: Article 2 of this Order amends article 12 of the National Bus Travel Concession Scheme for Older and Disabled Persons (Scotland) Order 2006 to set the capped level of funding and the reimbursement rate for the financial year 2025/2026.

items.title: Norwich Livestock Market Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision for the relocation of Norwich Livestock Market; and for connected purposes.

items.title: General Cemetery Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make new provision for the regulation and management of the General Cemetery Company upon its registration under the Companies Act 2006; to permit the transfer of the ownership and management of Kensal Green Cemetery and West London Crematorium to a charity; and to confer powers upon the General Cemetery Company to extinguish rights of burial and disturb human remains in Kensal Green Cemetery for the purpose of increasing the space for interments; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Lords Spiritual (Women) Act 2015 (Extension) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to extend the period within which vacancies among the Lords Spiritual are to be filled by bishops who are women.

items.title: Terrorism (Protection of Premises) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to require persons with control of certain premises or events to take steps to reduce the vulnerability of the premises or event to, and the risk of physical harm to individuals arising from, acts of terrorism; to confer related functions on the Security Industry Authority; to limit the disclosure of information about licensed premises that is likely to be useful to a person committing or preparing an act of terrorism; and for connected purposes.

items.title: National Insurance Contributions (Secondary Class 1 Contributions) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about secondary Class 1 contributions.

items.title: Non-Domestic Rating (Multipliers and Private Schools) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision for, and in connection with, the introduction of higher non-domestic rating multipliers as regards large business hereditaments, and lower non-domestic rating multipliers as regards retail, hospitality and leisure hereditaments, in England and for the removal of charitable relief from non-domestic rates for private schools in England.

items.title: Steel Industry (Special Measures) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about powers to secure the continued and safe use of assets of a steel undertaking.

items.title: Institute for Apprenticeships and Technical Education (Transfer of Functions etc) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to transfer the functions of the Institute for Apprenticeships and Technical Education, and its property, rights and liabilities, to the Secretary of State; to abolish the Institute; and to make amendments relating to the transferred functions.

items.title: Bank Resolution (Recapitalisation) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about recapitalisation costs in relation to the special resolution regime under the Banking Act 2009.

items.title: Great British Energy Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about Great British Energy.

items.title: Sentencing Guidelines (Pre-sentence Reports) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about sentencing guidelines in relation to pre-sentence reports.

items.title: Data (Use and Access) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about access to customer data and business data; to make provision about services consisting of the use of information to ascertain and verify facts about individuals; to make provision about the recording and sharing, and keeping of registers, of information relating to apparatus in streets; to make provision about the keeping and maintenance of registers of births and deaths; to make provision for the regulation of the processing of information relating to identified or identifiable living individuals; to make provision about privacy and electronic communications; to establish the Information Commission; to make provision about information standards for health and social care; to make provision about the grant of smart meter communication licences; to make provision about the disclosure of information to improve public service delivery; to make provision about the retention of information by providers of internet services in connection with investigations into child deaths; to make provision about providing information for purposes related to the carrying out of independent research into online safety matters; to make provision about the retention of biometric data; to make provision about services for the provision of electronic signatures, electronic seals and other trust services; to make provision about works protected by copyright and the development of artificial intelligence systems; to make provision about the creation of purported intimate images; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Supply and Appropriation (Main Estimates) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the year ending with 31 March 2026; to authorise both the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund and the application of income for that year; and to appropriate the supply authorised for that year by this Act and by the Supply and Appropriation (Anticipation and Adjustments) Act 2025.

items.title: Commonwealth Parliamentary Association and International Committee of the Red Cross (Status) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about the status of, and privileges and immunities in connection with, the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association and the International Committee of the Red Cross; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Product Regulation and Metrology Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about the marketing or use of products in the United Kingdom; about units of measurement and the quantities in which goods are marketed in the United Kingdom; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Football Governance Act 2025
items.description: An Act to establish the Independent Football Regulator; to make provision for the licensing of football clubs; to make provision about the distribution of revenue received by organisers of football competitions; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Universal Credit Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision to alter the rates of the standard allowance, limited capability for work element and limited capability for work and work-related activity element of universal credit and the rates of income-related employment and support allowance.

items.title: Armed Forces Commissioner Act 2025
items.description: An Act to establish, and confer functions on, the Armed Forces Commissioner; to abolish the office of Service Complaints Ombudsman; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Bus Services Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about local and school bus services; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Deprivation of Citizenship Orders (Effect during Appeal) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about the effect, during an appeal, of an order under section 40 of the British Nationality Act 1981.

items.title: Renters’ Rights Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision changing the law about rented homes, including provision abolishing fixed term assured tenancies and assured shorthold tenancies; imposing obligations on landlords and others in relation to rented homes and temporary and supported accommodation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Absent Voting (Elections in Scotland and Wales) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about absent voting in connection with local government elections in Scotland and Wales, elections to the Scottish Parliament and elections to Senedd Cymru; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Public Authorities (Fraud, Error and Recovery) Act 2025
items.description: An Act to make provision about the prevention of fraud against public authorities and the making of erroneous payments by public authorities; about the recovery of money paid by public authorities as a result of fraud or error; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Property (Digital Assets etc) Act 2025
